About the Foundation

Bringing Vedanta to
the Seeking World

Aham Brahmaasmi Foundation® is a unit of Dakshinamnaya Sri Sharada Peetham, Sringeri — one of the four cardinal Peethams established by Jagadguru Sri Adi Shankaracharya Bhagavatpada over twelve centuries ago.

The Foundation carries the timeless Mahavakya "Aham Brahmaasmi — I am Brahman" as its guiding light, seeking to acquaint sincere seekers worldwide with the Upanishadic philosophy of Advaita in its pure and authentic form.

Under the divine grace of Jagadguru Shankaracharya Sri Sri Bharati Tirtha Mahasannidhanam and the direct guidance of Jagadguru Sri Sri Vidhushekhara Bharati Sannidhanam, the Foundation conducts immersive Vedanta programmes across India and internationally — in eight languages.

Divine Blessings

The Acharya Parampara

The Foundation operates under the blessings of the Jagadgurus of Sringeri — the living torch-bearers of the Advaita Vedanta tradition established by Sri Adi Shankaracharya.

36th Jagadguru Shankaracharya
Sri Sri Bharati Tirtha
Mahasannidhanam

Dakshinamnaya Sri Sharada Peetham, Sringeri

Jagadguru · Direct & Divine Guidance
Sri Sri Vidhushekhara Bharati
Sannidhanam

Dakshinamnaya Sri Sharada Peetham, Sringeri

Programme Details

Vedanta Shravana Shibira 2026

An immersive retreat grounded in authentic Vedantic discourse, Shravana, Manana, and Nididhyasana — as laid out in the Shastras and propagated by the seers of Bharatiya Sanatana Parampara.

Sringeri —
Where Dharma Lives

The sacred town where Adi Shankaracharya established his first Peetham, nestled in the forests of the Western Ghats.

Registration

Seek the Wisdom.
Register Today.

Fill the form to register your interest in the Vedanta Shravana Shibira 2026. The Foundation's team will reach out to confirm your participation and share further details.

All registrations are handled with the utmost reverence and care. Your details will be used solely for programme coordination.

श्रवणं कीर्तनं विष्णोः स्मरणं पादसेवनम् ।
अर्चनं वन्दनं दास्यं सख्यमात्मनिवेदनम् ॥ — Bhagavata Purana, 7.5.23
